body_txt: If a TV character wants cosmetic surgery, they will be inevitably convinced by the end of the episode that they're beautiful "just the way they are" and the audience gets An Aesop about loving yourself. A subtrope of Be Yourself . Compare Plastic Bitch , when a character who has had plastic surgery is treated as a bad person — presumably because they never learned to embrace their natural beauty. Should they go through with it anyway, the convincer will likely think of it as an Unnecessary Makeover ; whether the audience does as well is subjective. See Fans Prefer the New Her when the audience disagrees with this trope. Also contrast Beautiful All Along , where a character becomes more attractive after changing clothes, and I Can Change My Beloved , where a character believes they can change another's behavior for the better. For the film of the same name, see here .
